The '''61st British Academy Film Awards''', more commonly known as the [[BAFTAs]], took place on 10 February 2008 at the [[Royal Albert Hall]] in [[London]], honouring the best national and foreign films of [[2007 in film|2007]]. Presented by the [[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]], accolades were handed out for the best feature-length film and documentaries of any nationality that were screened at British cinemas in 2007.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/culture/film/3671093/Baftas-2008-Atonement-wins-top-prize.html|title=Baftas 2008: Atonement wins top prize|date=10 February 2008|work=[[The Daily Telegraph|The Telegraph]]|access-date=11 February 2024}}</ref>

The nominees were announced on 16 January 2008 by [[Naomie Harris]] and [[Kelly Reilly]].<ref name="Nominations">{{cite web |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/orange-british-academy-film-awards-in-2008-the-nominations|title=Orange British Academy Film Awards in 2008: The nominations|date=16 January 2008|work=[[BAFTA]]|access-date=11 February 2024}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://variety.com/2008/film/awards/atonement-leads-bafta-longlist-1117978434/|title='Atonement' leads BAFTA longlist|date=4 January 2008|work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]|access-date=11 February 2024}}</ref> ''[[Atonement (2007 film)|Atonement]]'' won [[BAFTA Award for Best Film|Best Film]], while [[Coen brothers]], Joel and Ethan, won Best Director for ''[[No Country for Old Men]]'', which also went on to win Best Cinematography and Best Supporting Actor for [[Javier Bardem]]. [[Daniel Day-Lewis]] won Best Actor for ''[[There Will Be Blood]]'', [[Marion Cotillard]] won Best Actress for ''[[La Vie en Rose (film)|La Vie en Rose (La Môme)]]'', and [[Tilda Swinton]] won Best Supporting Actress for ''[[Michael Clayton (film)|Michael Clayton]]''. ''[[This Is England]]'', directed by [[Shane Meadows]], was voted Outstanding British Film of 2007.

[[Jonathan Ross]] hosted the ceremony for the second consecutive year.
